theory.intro: Many real situations contain a right-angled triangle. Once you spot it, decide whether the unknown is the hypotenuse or a shorter side, then apply Pythagoras' theorem. Give an exact answer or round as the question asks.
facts: The **hypotenuse** is the longest side and lies opposite the right angle.
To find the **hypotenuse**, add the squares: $h=\sqrt{a^{2}+b^{2}}$.
To find a **shorter side**, subtract the squares: $a=\sqrt{h^{2}-b^{2}}$.
A **Pythagorean triad** is three whole numbers with $a^{2}+b^{2}=c^{2}$, such as $3, 4, 5$; the largest is the hypotenuse.
Round only when asked; leave a surd such as $\sqrt{75}$ for an exact answer and do not simplify it unless told.
steps: Identify the right-angled triangle and the two known sides.
Decide whether the unknown is the hypotenuse or a shorter side.
Apply the matching formula and round only if required.

foundation.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.7]\coordinate (A) at (0,0);\coordinate (B) at (2,0);\coordinate (C) at (0,4.3);\draw (A)--(B) node[midway,below,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$1.9\text{ m}$};\draw (C)--(A) node[midway,left,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$x$};\draw (B)--(C) node[midway,above right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$4.5\text{ m}$};\draw (0.3,0)--(0.3,0.3)--(0,0.3);\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
A $4.5\text{ m}$ ladder leans against a wall with its foot $1.9\text{ m}$ from the base of the wall. How high up the wall does the ladder reach? Round to $1$ decimal place.
foundation.solution_text:
The ladder is the hypotenuse, so the height reached is a shorter side.
$x=\sqrt{4.5^{2}-1.9^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{16.64}$
$\approx4.1\text{ m}$

foundation.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.6]\coordinate (A) at (0,0);\coordinate (B) at (4,0);\coordinate (C) at (4,3.2);\draw (A)--(B) node[midway,below,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$18\text{ m}$};\draw (B)--(C) node[midway,right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$x$};\draw (C)--(A) node[midway,above left,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$30\text{ m}$};\draw (3.7,0)--(3.7,0.3)--(4,0.3);\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
A kite is flown on a $30\text{ m}$ string. The kite is directly above a point $18\text{ m}$ from the person holding the string. How high is the kite above the ground?
foundation.solution_text:
The string is the hypotenuse, so the height is a shorter side.
$x=\sqrt{30^{2}-18^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{576}$
$=24\text{ m}$

foundation.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=1.2]\coordinate (A) at (0,0);\coordinate (B) at (3,0);\coordinate (C) at (3,2.25);\coordinate (D) at (0,2.25);\draw (A)--(B)--(C)--(D)--cycle;\draw (A)--(C) node[midway,above left,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$x$};\draw (A)--(B) node[midway,below,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$1.2\text{ m}$};\draw (B)--(C) node[midway,right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$0.9\text{ m}$};\draw (2.7,0)--(2.7,0.3)--(3,0.3);\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
A rectangular gate is $1.2\text{ m}$ wide and $0.9\text{ m}$ high. A diagonal brace runs corner to corner. How long is the brace?
foundation.solution_text:
The brace is the hypotenuse of the right-angled triangle.
$x=\sqrt{1.2^{2}+0.9^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{2.25}$
$=1.5\text{ m}$

foundation.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.45]\coordinate (A) at (0,0);\coordinate (B) at (5,0);\coordinate (C) at (0,12);\draw (A)--(B) node[midway,below,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$5\text{ m}$};\draw (A)--(C) node[midway,left,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$x$};\draw (B)--(C) node[midway,above right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$13\text{ m}$};\draw (0.6,0)--(0.6,0.6)--(0,0.6);\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
A guy wire $13\text{ m}$ long runs from the top of a vertical flagpole to a point on the ground $5\text{ m}$ from the base of the pole. How tall is the flagpole?
foundation.solution_text:
The wire is the hypotenuse, so the pole height is a shorter side.
$x=\sqrt{13^{2}-5^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{144}$
$=12\text{ m}$

foundation.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.05]\coordinate (A) at (0,0);\coordinate (B) at (90,0);\coordinate (C) at (90,45);\coordinate (D) at (0,45);\draw (A)--(B)--(C)--(D)--cycle;\draw (A)--(C) node[midway,above left,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$x$};\draw (A)--(B) node[midway,below,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$90\text{ m}$};\draw (B)--(C) node[midway,right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$45\text{ m}$};\draw (84,0)--(84,6)--(90,6);\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
A rectangular sports field is $90\text{ m}$ long and $45\text{ m}$ wide. How far is it to walk diagonally across the field? Round to $1$ decimal place.
foundation.solution_text:
The diagonal is the hypotenuse of the right-angled triangle.
$x=\sqrt{90^{2}+45^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{10125}$
$\approx100.6\text{ m}$

development.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.6]\coordinate (A) at (0,0);\coordinate (B) at (3,0);\coordinate (C) at (3,6.85);\draw[dashed] (A)--(B) node[midway,below,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$25\text{ m}$};\draw (B)--(C) node[midway,right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$x$};\draw (C)--(A) node[midway,above left,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$62.3\text{ m}$};\draw (2.7,0)--(2.7,0.3)--(3,0.3);\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
A straight rope $62.3\text{ m}$ long is stretched from the top of a short building to the top of a taller building. The buildings stand $25\text{ m}$ apart on level ground. Find $x$, the difference in height between the two buildings. Round to $1$ decimal place.
development.solution_text:
The rope is the hypotenuse, so the height difference is a shorter side.
$x=\sqrt{62.3^{2}-25^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{3256.29}$
$\approx57.1\text{ m}$

development.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.8]\draw (-0.5,0)--(5,0);\draw (0,0)--(0,2.6) node[midway,left,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$7.2\text{ m}$};\draw (4,0)--(4,3.5) node[midway,right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$9.6\text{ m}$};\draw (0,2.6)--(4,3.5) node[midway,above,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$x$};\draw[dashed] (0,2.6)--(4,2.6);\draw (3.7,2.6)--(3.7,2.9)--(4,2.9);\node[below] at (2,0) {$11\text{ m}$};\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Two posts $7.2\text{ m}$ and $9.6\text{ m}$ tall stand $11\text{ m}$ apart on level ground. Find the length $x$ of the string stretched from the top of one post to the top of the other. Round to $1$ decimal place.
development.solution_text:
The string is the hypotenuse. The vertical side is the difference in the post heights.
$9.6-7.2=2.4\text{ m}$
$x=\sqrt{11^{2}+2.4^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{126.76}$
$\approx11.3\text{ m}$

development.question_text: The numbers $20$, $99$ and $x$ form a Pythagorean triad, where $x$ is the largest number. Find the value of $x$.
development.solution_text:
In a Pythagorean triad the largest number is the hypotenuse.
$x=\sqrt{20^{2}+99^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{10201}$
$=101$

[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.9]\coordinate (A) at (0,0);\coordinate (B) at (5.4,0);\coordinate (C) at (5.4,3.8);\coordinate (D) at (0,3.8);\draw (A)--(B)--(C)--(D)--cycle;\draw (A)--(C) node[midway,above left,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$x$};\draw (A)--(B) node[midway,below,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$5.4\text{ m}$};\draw (B)--(C) node[midway,right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$3.8\text{ m}$};\draw (5.1,0)--(5.1,0.3)--(5.4,0.3);\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
A rectangular room measures $5.4\text{ m}$ by $3.8\text{ m}$. Find the length of the diagonal of the room. Round to $1$ decimal place.
development.solution_text:
The diagonal is the hypotenuse of the right-angled triangle.
$x=\sqrt{5.4^{2}+3.8^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{43.6}$
$\approx6.6\text{ m}$

development.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.9]\coordinate (A) at (0,0);\coordinate (B) at (4.5,0);\coordinate (C) at (0,0.6);\draw (A)--(B) node[midway,below,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$4.5\text{ m}$};\draw (A)--(C) node[midway,left,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$0.6\text{ m}$};\draw (C)--(B) node[midway,above,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$x$};\draw (0.2,0)--(0.2,0.2)--(0,0.2);\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
A wheelchair ramp rises $0.6\text{ m}$ over a horizontal distance of $4.5\text{ m}$. Find the length $x$ of the sloping ramp surface. Round to $2$ decimal places.
development.solution_text:
The ramp surface is the hypotenuse of the right-angled triangle.
$x=\sqrt{4.5^{2}+0.6^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{20.61}$
$\approx4.54\text{ m}$

mastery.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.7]\coordinate (A) at (0,0);\coordinate (B) at (5,0);\coordinate (C) at (2.5,4.33);\coordinate (M) at (2.5,0);\draw (A)--(B)--(C)--cycle;\draw[dashed] (C)--(M) node[midway,right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$x$};\draw (A)--(C) node[midway,above left,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$10\text{ cm}$};\draw (B)--(C) node[midway,above right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$10\text{ cm}$};\node[below] at (1.25,0) {$5\text{ cm}$};\node[below] at (3.75,0) {$5\text{ cm}$};\draw (2.5,0.28)--(2.78,0.28)--(2.78,0);\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Find the perpendicular height $x$ of an equilateral triangle with side length $10\text{ cm}$. Give your answer in exact surd form.
mastery.solution_text:
The height splits the triangle into two right-angled triangles, each with base $5\text{ cm}$.
$x=\sqrt{10^{2}-5^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{75}\text{ cm}$

mastery.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.6]\coordinate (A) at (0,0);\coordinate (B) at (6,0);\coordinate (C) at (6,3);\coordinate (D) at (0,3);\draw (A)--(B)--(C)--(D)--cycle;\draw (A)--(C) node[midway,above,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$10\text{ cm}$};\draw (A)--(B) node[midway,below,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$2x$};\draw (B)--(C) node[midway,right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$x$};\draw (5.7,0)--(5.7,0.3)--(6,0.3);\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
The diagonal of a rectangle is $10\text{ cm}$ long. The rectangle is twice as long as it is wide. Find the dimensions of the rectangle. Round to $2$ decimal places.
mastery.solution_text:
Let the width be $x$, so the length is $2x$.
$x^{2}+(2x)^{2}=10^{2}$
$5x^{2}=100$
$x^{2}=20$
$x=\sqrt{20}$
The rectangle is $\sqrt{20}\text{ cm}\approx4.47\text{ cm}$ wide and $2\sqrt{20}\text{ cm}\approx8.94\text{ cm}$ long.

mastery.question_text: A triangle is to be constructed with two of its sides measuring $18\text{ cm}$ and $22\text{ cm}$, and it must be right-angled. There are two possible lengths for the third side. Find both. Round to $2$ decimal places.
mastery.solution_text:
The two given sides could both be shorter sides, or the longer one could be the hypotenuse.
Case 1: $18$ and $22$ are both shorter sides, so the third side is the hypotenuse.
$x=\sqrt{18^{2}+22^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{808}$
$\approx28.43\text{ cm}$
Case 2: $22$ is the hypotenuse, so the third side is a shorter side.
$x=\sqrt{22^{2}-18^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{160}$
$\approx12.65\text{ cm}$
